back_translation: And David went up from there and stayed in the strongholds of En-gedi.
And it happened, when Saul returned from after the Philistines, that they told him, saying, 'Behold, David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.'
And Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel and went to seek David and his men along the rocks of the wild goats.
And he came to the sheepfolds of the flocks by the road, and there was a cave. And Saul came to relieve his feet, and David and his men were sitting in the recesses of the cave.

And David's men said to him, 'Behold, the day of which the LORD said to you, Behold, I am giving your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it is good in your eyes.' And David arose and cut off the corner of the robe that belonged to Saul stealthily.
And it happened after this, that the heart of David struck him, because he cut off the corner that belonged to Saul.
And he said to his men, 'Far be it from me from the LORD, that I should do this thing to my lord, to the LORD's anointed, to send out my hand against him, for the LORD's anointed is he.'
And David restrained his men with the words, and did not allow them to rise up against Saul. And Saul arose from the cave and went on the way.
And David arose after this, and went out of the cave, and called after Saul, saying, 'My lord the king.' And Saul looked behind him, and David bowed face down to the ground and prostrated himself.
And David said to Saul, 'Why do you listen to the words of men, saying, Behold, David is seeking your harm?

Behold, this day your eyes have seen that the LORD gave you today into my hand in the cave, and someone said to kill you, but I had compassion on you, and I said, I will not send my hand against my lord, for he is the LORD's anointed.

And my father, look, even look at the corner of your robe in my hand, for when I cut off the corner of your robe and did not kill you, know and see that there is no evil or rebellion in my hand, and I have not sinned against you, but you are hunting my soul to take it.
May the LORD judge between me and you, and may the LORD take vengeance for me from you, but my hand will not be against you.
As the proverb of the ancients says, From the wicked comes forth wickedness. But my hand will not be against you.
After whom has the king of Israel come out? After whom are you pursuing? After a dead dog? After one flea?
And may the LORD be judge, and judge between me and you, and may He see and plead my cause, and judge me from your hand.'
And it happened, when David finished speaking these words to Saul, that Saul said, 'Is this your voice, my son David?' And Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
And he said to David, 'You are more righteous than I, for you have rewarded me with good, but I have rewarded you with evil.
And you have declared this day what you did with me of good — that the LORD closed me in your hand and you did not kill me.
And when a man finds his enemy, will he send him on a good way? May the LORD reward you with good in place of this day, for what you have done for me.
And now, behold, I know that you will surely reign, and the kingdom of Israel will stand in your hand.
And now, swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my seed after me, and that you will not destroy my name from the house of my father.'
And David swore to Saul, and Saul went to his house, and David and his men went up to the stronghold.
